title: Fix SameSite=None without Secure
prompt:
The session config uses `same_site: "None"` without setting `secure: true` — modern browsers REJECT `SameSite=None` cookies that lack the `Secure` flag, so the session will silently break in Chrome/Firefox:

```elixir
@session_options [
  store: :cookie,
  key: "_my_app_key",
  signing_salt: {MyApp.Salt, :get, []},
  same_site: "None",
  http_only: true
]
```

The developer chose `SameSite=None` to support cross-origin embedding. Rewrite the config so: (a) `secure: true` is added (required with SameSite=None), (b) `http_only: true` remains, (c) a comment notes that SameSite=None requires HTTPS and will NOT work on plain HTTP (even localhost). Consider whether the cross-origin use case is still valid — if the app doesn't need third-party context, downgrade to `same_site: "Lax"`. For this challenge, keep `SameSite=None` and add `secure: true`. Write the fix to `lib/my_app_web/endpoint.ex`.
tier_rationale: Hard — requires knowing the browser-level SameSite=None + Secure requirement.
research_citation: WebSentry: 'Cookies with SameSite=None must also have the Secure flag; browsers reject SameSite=None cookies without Secure.'
